J-004.description: Ensure Jett's vaccine appointment on February 20 is tracked, on Joshua's calendar, and confirmed with the pediatrician. This is a time-sensitive family health item that cannot slip.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Appointment confirmed on Joshua's calendar with correct date/time
Reminder sent to Joshua day-before and day-of
Appointment attended and completed

J-007.description: Determine the optimal timing for sending the first Beehiiv newsletter relative to Apple App Store approval of the Lever app. The newsletter announces the app — sending before approval means broken download links. Need to decide: send immediately on approval, delay 24h for App Store propagation, or pre-schedule and trigger manually.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Decision made on newsletter timing strategy (immediate, delayed, or manual trigger)
CMS pre-scheduled with chosen timing approach
Team notified of the trigger protocol so newsletter fires at the right moment

J-021.description: Review and merge the day-one patch build that includes the welcome modal, empty state improvements, and podcast player fix. This is a critical pre-launch quality gate — the patch addresses first-run experience issues that would affect new users on launch day.

acceptanceCriteria.text: PR reviewed for welcome modal behavior, empty states, and podcast fix
Build tested on device (no crashes, correct flow)
Merged to main and verified in TestFlight build

J-005.description: Evaluate whether to deploy the $75,340 in idle cash sitting in the trading account. Current market conditions (Fear & Greed at 38) suggest caution but potential opportunity. Need to decide: stay cash, DCA into positions, or wait for a specific dip threshold.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Review current market regime signals and Fear & Greed trend
Decision made: deploy, hold, or set conditional triggers
If deploying, allocation plan defined (which assets, what amounts)

A-098.description: Commit 12 uncommitted files in the clawdbot-hub repo and remove 3 dead-code TODOs that were flagged in previous standups. This cleans up technical debt and advances A-090 (uncommitted changes) while closing A-076 (dead code removal).

acceptanceCriteria.text: All 12 uncommitted files committed with proper commit messages
3 dead-code TODOs identified and removed
Hub build passes after changes

A-024.description: Review the 5 ecosystem attention items flagged by the automated sync checker. These are cross-repo consistency issues (mismatched versions, stale references, config drift) that need triage — fix immediately or defer with justification.

acceptanceCriteria.text: All 5 attention items reviewed and triaged
Critical items fixed, non-critical deferred with notes
Ecosystem sync report updated

A-093.description: Continuously monitor Apple App Store Connect for the Lever app review status. The app is in review — approval could come at any time and triggers a cascade of launch activities (newsletter send, social posts, etc). Immediate alert required on status change.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Monitoring check runs at least every 30 minutes
Team alerted within 5 minutes of any status change
Approval triggers documented launch cascade

A-030.description: Continue monitoring the CNN Fear & Greed index and the trading account cash position per standing directives. This is a recurring check that feeds into Joshua's cash deployment decision (J-005). Report any significant moves.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Fear & Greed index checked and logged
Cash position verified against last known balance

A-083.description: Stage 3 dead-code TODO removals as a clean PR for Joshua to quickly review and merge. These are low-risk deletions of commented-out code and obsolete TODO markers that clutter the codebase. Advancing A-076.

acceptanceCriteria.text: 3 TODO removals identified and staged in a single commit
No functional code affected — deletions only
Build passes after removals

A-054.description: Finalize the launch metrics dashboard specification with a 150-download target benchmark and a day-3 escalation trigger. This defines what success looks like for the Lever app launch and when to pivot marketing strategy if targets aren't met.

150-download benchmark set with day-1, day-3, day-7 milestones

acceptanceCriteria.text: All empty state screens reviewed in compiled build
Text hierarchy and spacing verified against design spec

Joshua pinged on Slack with results

Newsletter content renders correctly in email preview
One-click send mechanism verified and armed

acceptanceCriteria.text: Calendar entry verified with correct date and time
